title: Duty of care
summary: 9084 Tort — neighbour principle, Caparo three-stage test, and established duties.
learningObjectives: Explain Donoghue v Stevenson neighbour principle.
Analyse established duties: road users, employers, manufacturers.
Evaluate policy limits on duty of care.

simpleExplanation.title: Who owes whom a duty?
simpleExplanation.summary: A duty of care exists when harm is foreseeable, parties are sufficiently connected, and it is fair to impose liability — Caparo refines the neighbour test.
steps: Established categories — easier to prove duty.

sections.content: **Duty of care** (4.1.2) — **Caparo three-stage** test for novel situations.

sections.label: Mark a duty of care question
